title: dispatch show --text: reconstruct readable agent narrative from session events

tasks.title: Implement event text reconstruction logic in dispatch business logic package
tasks.description:
Create a new function `reconstructSessionText` in `src/dispatch/` (or the appropriate business logic package that handles dispatch/session operations — check existing files like `src/dispatch/session.ts` or similar).

The function should:
- Accept an array of ACP session events (read from `.telesis/sessions/<id>.events.jsonl`)
- Iterate over events in sequence order
- Concatenate `text` fields from events where `type === 'output'` into a running text buffer
- When a tool call event is encountered (check for `type === 'tool_call'` or `type === 'tool_use'` — inspect existing event type definitions to confirm the shape), emit an inline marker like `\n[tool: <tool_name>]\n` followed by any result text if present
- When a thinking event is encountered (`type === 'thinking'`), emit `[thinking...]` as a marker
- Return the fully reconstructed string

Before writing new code:
- Inspect `src/dispatch/` and related files to understand the existing event type definitions and session reading utilities
- Reuse any existing `readSessionEvents` or similar helpers rather than duplicating JSONL parsing
- Check `src/daemon/events.ts` or wherever ACP event types are defined to get the exact shape of output, tool, and thinking events

Write colocated unit tests in `src/dispatch/reconstruct.test.ts` (or adjacent to wherever you place the function). Tests must cover:
- Pure output events concatenated correctly
- Tool call events rendered inline with their name
- Thinking events rendered as `[thinking...]`
- Mixed sequence of all three types produces coherent output
- Empty event array returns empty string
- Events with missing or undefined `text` fields do not crash
validationErrors: Create reconstructSessionText function in src/dispatch/ package
Function accepts an array of ACP session events
Concatenates text fields from output events into a running buffer
Emits inline marker for tool call events with tool name and optional result text
Reuses existing event type definitions (inspect src/dispatch/, src/daemon/events.ts)
Write colocated unit tests in src/dispatch/reconstruct.test.ts
Tests cover pure output events concatenated correctly
Tests cover tool call events rendered inline with their name
Tests cover thinking events rendered as [thinking...]
Tests cover mixed sequence of all three types produces coherent output
Tests cover empty event array returns empty string
Tests cover events with missing or undefined text fields do not crash

tasks.title: Add --text flag to `telesis dispatch show` CLI command
tasks.description:
Modify the `telesis dispatch show` command in `src/cli/dispatch.ts` (or wherever the dispatch CLI commands are defined — find the file that registers the `dispatch show` subcommand with Commander).

Changes required:
- Add `.option('--text', 'Reconstruct and display full agent narrative from session events')` to the `show` command definition
- In the action handler, when `options.text` is true:
  1. Read the session's `.events.jsonl` file (reuse existing session loading logic)
  2. Call the `reconstructSessionText` function from task-1
  3. Print the reconstructed text to stdout
- When `options.text` is false/absent, the existing behavior must be completely unchanged
- If the session events file does not exist or is empty, print a helpful message like `No events found for session <id>` and exit cleanly (no stack trace)
- If reconstruction produces an empty string (e.g., session has no output events), print `No output events in session <id>`

Do not modify the default code path in any way. The `--text` branch is purely additive.
